new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) in containers > 2 L and ≤ 10 L (subheading 2204 22 79)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 bis einschließlich 10 Litern (Unterposition 2204 22 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) within the meaning of additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres (subheading 2204 22 79).

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 79 are: (1) wine of fresh grapes, (2) bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) per additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, (3) presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres, and (4) still (non-sparkling and non-semi-sparkling). Criteria (1)–(3) are fully captured: the class is a subclass of WineOfFreshGrapes2204, bears ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality, and has part a LargeContainer (≥ 2 L). The LargeContainer TBox class covers ≥ 2 L; the upper bound of ≤ 10 L for subheading 2204 22 is not separately modelled in the TBox (no 'MediumContainer' class exists) — this is a gap reducing coverage. The still-wine criterion is captured via complement restrictions on SparklingWineQuality and SemiSparklingPressureQuality. The precise content of additional note 6(a) (definition of PGI) is not axiomatised beyond referencing the existing ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality quality class, which is a reasonable level of coverage given the TBox vocabulary available. Overall coverage score: 0.82 (gap: upper 10 L container-volume ceiling not expressible with existing TBox classes).
